qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: User enumeration via distinct error responses in magic-link send-link endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unauthenticated user enumeration in POST /auth/send-link via distinct email lookup responses; the advisory concerns authorization bypass in /api/admin/* routes caused by Gin admin middleware detecting a forbidden non-admin user but failing to abort the handler chain.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The only qualifying finding is an unauthenticated /auth/send-link response discrepancy, while GHSA-xfx2-prg5-jq3g is an authenticated non-admin authorization bypass in /api/admin/* endpoints due to a missing c.Abort() in the Gin admin middleware.
evidence: Advisory summary and details describe /api/admin/* authorization bypass where standard authenticated users receive FORBIDDEN but request processing continues because Gin admin middleware lacks c.Abort().
user_enumeration_via_sendlink/vulnerability_report.json identifies POST /auth/send-link, routes/auth/post_sendlink.go, and apierrors.EmailNotRegistered as the vulnerable path.
The run manifest lists user_enumeration_via_sendlink as the only qualifying_vuln final finding; array_oob_tutor_registration is non_qualifying_vuln.
